text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy village of Whispering Woods, there lived a curious hedgehog named Pippen. Every dawn Pippen would emerge from his prickly nest and venture out into the enchanting forest.

One sunny morning, Pippen noticed something unusual. Among the vibrant leaves and dancing butterflies, a large dark shadow flickered silently beside the sunlit path. He hopped closer and froze. It was a bear!

Pippen wasn't frightened. He knew he could outwit anything big. With a hop here and a leap there, he zigzagged across his usual route. The bear watched calmly, not picking prey.

Curiosity got the better of Pippen. He scampered up the mossy trunk of an old oak. There stood a magnificent eagle owl! Pippen knew big birds could seem scary, but this one didn't seem mean.

"Why do you sit here so late?" pippen asked.

"To watch over my nest," said the owl in a booming voice
